spec_match.rationale: The timeline covers click, browser prompt, activate with `event.target.stream`, error, stream assignment, stop, and second-click recovery paths.

interactive.rationale: Visitors can run the granted path, pick one of four DOMException error variants, simulate the selected failure, replay the recovery click, and reset the timeline.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The animated timeline and event log now cover both successful activation and differentiated failure/recovery branches.

edge_cases.rationale: NotAllowedError, NotFoundError, OverconstrainedError, SecurityError, and retry-after-error behavior are all represented with specific recovery guidance.

design_system.rationale: New controls use existing CSS variables and remove the previous glyph-prefixed button labels.

summary: Resolved: the event lifecycle demo now differentiates the four relevant DOMException names, shows a second-click recovery path, logs `event.target.stream`, and includes the ChromeStatus self-heal link.
